mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate elevated above 3.5% signals monetary tightening that increases discount rates on equity valuations. Higher FEDFUNDS directly compresses forward equity returns through reduced present value of future cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 3.5%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer purchasing power. A 0.3 percentage point increase in UNRATE over 3 months predicts negative equity returns as earnings expectations decline and recession risk rises, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market deterioration
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances, or correlation between UNRATE changes and forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts measured by HOUST decline below 1000 thousand units signals weakening residential demand and construction activity. This contraction in housing demand predicts broader economic slowdown and negative equity returns over the next 5 trading days as forward guidance deteriorates.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1006.0 thousand units as of 2008-01-15
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive in > 58% of instances when HOUST < 1000, or Rank IC between HOUST and 5-day returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index INDPRO and payroll employment PAYEMS jointly signal real economic capacity and labor utilization. When both INDPRO growth slows below 0.5% monthly and PAYEMS growth falls below 50 thousand monthly additions, the interaction indicates demand destruction that produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO monthly growth < 0.5% AND PAYEMS monthly growth < 50 thousand
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 114.23 and PAYEMS at 138102.0 as of 2008-01-15
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in > 62% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction term (INDPRO × PAYEMS) has Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales measured by RSAFS decline month-over-month, signaling weakening consumer demand and reduced discretionary spending. A 1% or greater monthly decline in RSAFS predicts negative equity returns over 60 trading days as consumer-driven earnings expectations contract.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 382906.0 million dollars as of 2008-01-15
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in > 65% of triggered instances, or Spearman rank correlation between RSAFS changes and forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.12).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL and monetary policy rate FEDFUNDS interact to determine real interest rates. When CPIAUCSL rises above 211.5 while FEDFUNDS remains below 4.0%, the real rate compression signals monetary accommodation that produces positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 211.68 and FEDFUNDS at 3.94% as of 2008-01-15
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with rising inflation
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in > 58% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction term (CPIAUCSL × FEDFUNDS) has Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment PAYEMS measured as a trend signal indicates labor market momentum. Sustained month-over-month growth in PAYEMS above 100 thousand persons signals robust labor demand and consumer income growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as earnings expectations strengthen.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 138102.0 thousand persons as of 2008-01-15
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative in > 55% of all periods, or Rank IC between PAYEMS growth and 5-day returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.15) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth GDPC1 and housing starts HOUST jointly measure aggregate demand momentum. When GDPC1 annualized growth falls below 2.0% while HOUST simultaneously declines below 1050 thousand units, the interaction signals demand-side recession risk that produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth < 2.0% AND HOUST < 1050 thousand
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11677.4 billion dollars and HOUST at 1006.0 thousand units as of 2008-01-15
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction term (GDPC1 × HOUST) has Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window.
